description_long: This radio receiver features a rectangular body with a brushed metal top and front panel, accented by wooden side panels that give it a classic, vintage appearance. The front face is equipped with multiple rotary knobs, a large tuning dial, and a horizontal blue-lit frequency display, as well as a small analog meter on the left. The top surface has two sets of ventilation slits for heat dissipation. The rear panel includes a variety of input/output ports, speaker terminals, and connection points for audio equipment. The overall design is robust and functional, with a blend of metallic and wooden textures. The object is likely to be found in home audio setups, garages, or retro-themed urban environments.
description: A vintage-style radio receiver with a brushed metal front, wooden side panels, multiple control knobs, and a blue-lit frequency display. The rear features various audio input/output ports and speaker terminals.
description_view_0: The front view shows the brushed metal face with five rotary knobs, a large tuning dial, a blue-lit frequency display, and an analog meter.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the wooden panel and the protruding control knobs, with no additional features visible.
description_view_2: The rear view displays multiple audio input/output ports, speaker terminals, and connection points, all set in a black panel.
description_view_3: The right side view shows the plain wooden panel and the ends of the control knobs, with no other distinguishing features.
